questions.correct_text: It scores every token in its vocabulary and samples from those scores
It spreads the distribution across more tokens, making output more varied
Its candidate pool adapts in size — it adds tokens until their cumulative probability reaches p
Low temperature, because code must be syntactically correct and predictable
Sampling introduces randomness, so the token chosen at each step can differ each run

distractor_plausibility.note: All distractors map to credible beginner misconceptions.

mapping.question: q1
mapping.lo: Explain what a probability distribution over tokens is and how an LLM uses one at each generation step

mapping.question: q2
mapping.lo: Describe what temperature is and how changing it makes output more predictable or more varied

mapping.question: q3
mapping.lo: Distinguish between greedy decoding, top-k sampling, and top-p (nucleus) sampling

mapping.question: q4
mapping.lo: Select an appropriate temperature setting for a given task

mapping.question: q5

beginner_fair_scope.note: All stems use plain language. No question tests logits arithmetic, softmax math, or concepts beyond temperature/sampling mechanics. No prior-topic concepts invoked beyond what is needed.
